prompt: I have an idea for a small SaaS dashboard. Use the agile department workflow, but only give me the plan and do not create files yet.
expected: The skill should choose Plan-only mode, describe the control/product/engineering/review workflow, and avoid file creation.

prompt: Initialize the department workflow for this repo and prepare the first handoff so Product can write SPEC-001. Do not change source code.
expected: The skill should follow the bootstrap checklist, create or preserve .agent-workspace, handle Git exclusion, initialize status, and prepare a product handoff without touching source code.

prompt: Engineering wants to start coding from a draft Spec with no acceptance criteria. Continue the workflow.
expected: The skill should block coding, route the issue back to Product or Review, and record the missing acceptance criteria.

prompt: I want to build a task management tool. Generate the master plan.
expected: The skill should not generate master-plan.md yet. It should ask discovery questions about target user, core problem, core flow, MVP scope, success criteria, platform, and constraints.

prompt: The workspace has two active iterations and status.md says a different Spec is in code review. Continue the workflow.
expected: The skill should write or request a recovery-report.md, mark confidence low, enter blocked, and avoid silently repairing status.md.

prompt: .agent-workspace files are already tracked by Git. Bootstrap the workflow.
expected: The skill should STOP and ask for a user decision before continuing; it must not proceed as if local-only collaboration state is safe.

prompt: Audit the department workflow. active/iteration-current has no handoffs for the last review and archive has a directly edited old review.
expected: The skill should use Audit mode, report stale active work and archive mutation, and avoid modifying code.

prompt: Close iteration 002. Two stories are complete and one story is unfinished.
expected: The skill should write final-summary.md, archive completed evidence, return unfinished work to backlog, and not describe unfinished work as shipped.

prompt: Review found a blocking security issue in the implementation for SPEC-004.
expected: The skill should keep the workflow at code-review, route fixes to Engineering, and require updated verification before Product acceptance.

prompt: Product rejected acceptance because the implemented workflow does not match the approved acceptance criteria.
expected: The skill should not mark done. Control should decide whether this is Product clarification or Engineering correction and route accordingly.

prompt: Continue automatic department coordination, but no thread tool is available.
expected: The skill should switch to semi-automatic mode, write handoff files, and provide exact prompts for the user to paste into department conversations.

prompt: There are two active handoffs for Engineering that assign different Specs to the same working tree.
expected: The skill should stop concurrent engineering work, require Control to choose one active owner or define a branch/worktree strategy, and record the conflict.

prompt: Product discovery is complete. Directly choose a tech stack and generate the master plan.
expected: The skill should not choose a stack or generate master-plan.md. Engineering should ask technical discovery questions about new/existing project, platform, existing stack, data persistence, auth/permissions, deployment, integrations, scale, and non-functional constraints.

prompt: The product requirements are clear, but I do not know where this should be deployed or which database/auth stack to use.
expected: The skill should offer 2-4 concrete deployment/data/auth options, allow an explicit assumption only when labeled, and record matching risks before technical review.

prompt: Product and technical constraints are clear. Prepare the technical direction before master planning.
expected: Engineering should output feasibility plus architecture-options.md with Fast MVP, Production-Ready, and Minimal Dependency / Local-First options, then route to technical review.

prompt: Review found the proposed architecture is over-engineered and does not satisfy the deployment constraint.
expected: The workflow should keep or return to technical-review/technical-discovery, route changes to Engineering for revised architecture-options.md, and not enter master-planning.

prompt: I have an idea for an app, but I only know it should help freelancers manage client work. Start the workflow and ask me what you need.
expected: The skill should stay in product-discovery, ask only 1-3 high-impact product questions, use a structured user-input or multiple-choice tool when available, and avoid generating a master plan.

prompt: The product discovery is complete, but I am not technical. Pick the stack you think is best and continue.
expected: The skill should not silently pick a stack. Engineering should ask 1-3 technical questions or present 2-4 stack/deployment/data/auth options, record assumptions and risks, and wait before master-planning.
